pages.raw_text:
A special train on the Northern
Pacific will bring to Spokane the
middle of next week a delegation
of the chief magnates and officers
of the Chicago, Milwaukee & st.
Paul railway. They will make the
trip from Chicago to Butte, Mont,
over their own line and will travel
in the first train to be run over
portion of the road. They will
arrive in Butte Tuesday, and after
an inspection Of the terminals
there will go on to Spokane and
Seattle over the Northern Pacific.
1t is quite likely that the question
of Spokane terminal will be con-
sidered when the magnates are
here, as it is known that the Mil
waukee wants to enter this city.
The mode of entrance and ter
minal facilities are said to be as
yet unsettled, or If a selection has
been made, the ratification of such
matters may be considered.


The st. Paul Is essentially a
Standard Oil corporation, and Will
liam Rockefeller, brother of the
famous John D., it is said, will
head the st. Paul party on the
Pacific inspection tour. Other
members of the party are Roswell
Miller, chairman of the board of
directors of the st. Paul. the high
est official of the road; President
A. ]. Earling and George r. Peck
of Chicago, general counsel and
head of the legal department; Gen
eral Manager w. J. Underwood,
General Superintendent d. L..
Bush, d. J. Whittemore, chief engi-
neer; E. D. Sewall, president of
the ST Pauls roads in South Da-
kota and Montana; E. w. Adams,
secretary; E. S. Kelley, freight
traffic manager, Fl. S. Pierpont
general freight agent, and f. A
Miller, general passenge' agent.
